title: Falco Architecture & Detection Engine
description:
## Falco Architecture & Detection Engine

Falco is a cloud-native runtime security tool that monitors host and container activities.

How does the Falco engine intercept and monitor events occurring inside containerized workloads and on the host system?

Choose the correct option:

options.text: By parsing Kubernetes API audit logs as its primary and only source of system state.
By injecting a sidecar proxy into every pod to intercept IPC and network communications.
By monitoring system calls at the kernel level using a kernel module or eBPF probe.
By running periodic file integrity scans on the container filesystem layers.

explanation: Falco operates in kernel space (via a kernel module or eBPF probe) to trace system calls (syscalls) generated by processes on the host and inside containers. It sends these system calls to the user-space Falco daemon, which evaluates them against defined security rules in real-time.

hints.title: Falco Architecture Docs
hints.body: Falco uses eBPF or a kernel module to transparently tap into the system call stream from the Linux kernel.
